name: Medical Reasoning (MedXpertQA) — Local Solver
description: Prompt-engineer the system prompt for a LOCAL solver model (Qwen3.5-27B served on a remote GPU server via SSH tunnel) to answer fixed, seeded MedXpertQA multiple-choice questions. The prompt is optimized iteratively BY HAND: the working agent scores a prompt, reads the per-question failures, revises the system prompt, and re-scores — repeating for many rounds. Distinct from the sibling task that uses an API model as the solver — here the solver is local.
objective: Editing only the system prompt (a GENERAL reasoning strategy — NOT an answer key hardcoding specific dev answers): seal the baseline's held-out test number first (submit.py --baseline), iterate on dev EXACTLY 20 times (run_iter.py, selection by dev only), then seal the final test number (submit.py --prompt-file <best>) and write RESULTS.md reporting the baseline-vs-final test delta.
solver: Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B (local vLLM via a host SSH tunnel; reached from the Docker sandbox at http://host.docker.internal:8111/v1 by default — if that port moved, run check_tunnel.py to discover the live one)

dataset.subsample: fixed seed=42; dev n=20 (data.jsonl), test n=100 (data_test.jsonl)

entrypoint.iterate: python run_iter.py <file>   # by-hand loop: score dev, log the dashboard, print the misses to fix next (dev ONLY)

submit.cli: python submit.py --baseline   (before iterating)  |  python submit.py --prompt-file <best>   (after iteration 20)
